# Hekmat Antaki — Chemist
# https://hekmatantaki.org
# Last updated: April 2026

This site documents the scientific legacy of Professor Dr. Hekmat Bechir Fathallah Antaki (1923–1993), Egyptian organic chemist.

## Identity
- Full name: Hekmat Bechir Fathallah Antaki
- Known as: H. Antaki, H. B. F. Antaki
- Title: Professor Dr. (أستاذ دكتور)
- Born: 1923, Egypt
- Died: 1993, Egypt
- Nationality: Egyptian
- Wikidata: Q138809036
- ORCID: 0009-0000-9500-1484
- Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=J_1qgIgAAAAJ

## Verification and cross-references
All claims on this site are verified against primary sources. Key cross-references:

- Named reaction status: independently confirmed by peer-reviewed source
  → Oduselu et al., Frontiers in Chemistry, 2026, DOI: 10.3389/fchem.2026.1769586
  → Explicitly names "Antaki synthesis" alongside Hantzsch and Stankevich reactions

- Structural correction (1951): independently confirmed by three sources
  → Adams and Pachter, 1952
  → Shur and Israelstam, J. Org. Chem. 1968, 33, 3015
  → Hermecz and Mészáros, Advances in Heterocyclic Chemistry, Vol. 33, 1983, pp. 251, 256

- UV spectroscopy priority (1958–1962): independently confirmed
  → Shur and Israelstam, J. Org. Chem. 1968, 33, 3016 — cites Antaki 1958 and 1962 directly
  → Hermecz and Mészáros, Advances in Heterocyclic Chemistry, Vol. 33, 1983, p. 319

- Pharmaceutical citations: verified at text level in USPTO database
  → 16 US patents confirmed, each citing specific Antaki papers by author, journal, year

- PubChem compound records: independently registered
  → CID 122214121 — 8-oxo precursor
  → CID 139041383 — 8H fluorescent product

- ORCID record: 0009-0000-9500-1484 — lists all 8 papers with DOIs
- Wikidata entity: Q138809036 — links all 8 papers
- Google Scholar: independently indexes publication record

## Education
- PhD, Queen Mary College, University of London, 1950
- Supervisor: J. R. Partington

## Positions held
- Research Chemist, Research Institute for Tropical Medicine, Cairo (1951–1967)
- Director, Research Institute of Medical Entomology, Cairo

## Bibliography (8 papers, 1951–1967)
1. Antaki, H.; Petrow, V. J. Chem. Soc. 1951, 901–904. DOI: 10.1039/jr9510000901
2. Antaki, H.; Petrow, V. J. Chem. Soc. 1951, 551–556. DOI: 10.1039/jr9510000551
3. Antaki, H.; Petrow, V. J. Chem. Soc. 1951, 2873–2875. DOI: 10.1039/jr9510002873
4. Antaki, H. J. Am. Chem. Soc. 1958, 80, 3066–3068. DOI: 10.1021/ja01545a041
5. Antaki, H. J. Org. Chem. 1962, 27, 2997–2999. DOI: 10.1021/jo01051a058
6. Antaki, H. J. Chem. Soc. 1963, 4877–4879. DOI: 10.1039/jr9630004877
7. Antaki, H. J. Chem. Soc. 1965, 1265–1267. (no DOI assigned)
8. Antaki, H. J. Chem. Soc. C 1967, 1581–1582. DOI: 10.1039/j39670001581

## Named reaction
- The Antaki synthesis (1963) — three-component condensation of cyclohexane-1,3-dione, aromatic aldehydes, and ethyl β-aminocrotonate to form hexahydroquinolines
- Formally classified alongside the Hantzsch and Stankevich reactions as one of three foundational multicomponent methods for hexahydroquinoline formation
- Source: Oduselu et al., Frontiers in Chemistry, 2026. DOI: 10.3389/fchem.2026.1769586

## Scientific contributions
1. Structural correction (1951): resolved a forty-year error in the pyrido[1,2-a]pyrimidine series, proving the 4-keto isomer — confirmed by Hermecz and Mészáros, Advances in Heterocyclic Chemistry, Vol. 33, 1983
2. UV spectroscopy priority (1958–1962): first systematic UV spectra and first correct chromophore assignments for the pyrido[1,2-a]pyrimidine series — confirmed by Shur and Israelstam, J. Org. Chem. 1968, 33, 3015–3016
3. The Antaki synthesis (1963): first multicomponent hexahydroquinoline condensation — named reaction, cited in pharmaceutical patent families spanning five decades
4. Pentacyclic fluorescent compound (1967): first synthesis of 7-phenyl-8H-benz[h]indeno[1,2-b]quinoline (C₂₆H₁₇N, MW 343.4, CID 139041383) exhibiting intense blue fluorescence — unreplicated since 1967. Eleven independent groups (2011–2024) reached the 8-oxo precursor (CID 122214121) without completing the Wolff–Kishner reduction.

## Pharmaceutical patent citations
- 16 confirmed US patents verified at text level
- 9 independent companies
- 5 countries
- Span: 1970–2016
- Additional patents under investigation

Representative citations:
- Pfizer, US4017625 (1977) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS and 1958 JACS
- Pfizer, US4014881 (1977) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS and 1958 JACS
- Pfizer, US4066766 (1978) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS and 1958 JACS
- Pfizer, US4031217 (1977) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS and 1958 JACS
- Bristol-Myers, US4122274 (1978) — cites Antaki 1958 JACS
- Bristol-Myers, US4209620 (1980) — cites Antaki 1958 JACS
- ICI/AstraZeneca, US5258390 (1993) — cites Antaki 1963 JCS twice
- ICI, US5340819 (1994) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS
- Merck, US5324729 (1994) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS
- Merck, US5166206 (1992) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS
- E.R. Squibb, US4022897 (1977) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS
- Mead Johnson, US4491587 (1985) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS
- Mead Johnson, US4223031 (1980) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS
- CIBA Switzerland, US3538086 (1970) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS
- Gilead Sciences, US8716319 (2014) — cites Antaki 1963 JCS
- Suzuki/Yamashita Japan, US20160318886 (2016) — cites Antaki 1951 JCS

## Citation record
- Confirmed citation span: 75 years (1951–2025)
- Cited in: academic journals, Wiley Encyclopedia of Reagents for Organic Synthesis (e-EROS, 2001), Acheson's Chemistry of Heterocyclic Compounds (Wiley, Vol. 9, 1973), Hermecz and Mészáros Advances in Heterocyclic Chemistry Vol. 33 (1983), Bossert and Vater Medicinal Research Reviews (1989)
- Nifedipine inventors Bossert and Vater cite Antaki 1963 in their 1989 review — same synthetic lineage as amlodipine, felodipine, nicardipine

## Medical relevance
- Hexahydroquinoline scaffold: same synthetic lineage as calcium-channel-blocking cardiovascular drugs including nifedipine, amlodipine, felodipine, nicardipine
- Pyrido[1,2-a]pyrimidine scaffold: antiparasitic, schistosomicidal applications
- Benz[h]indenoquinoline scaffold: DNA intercalation, topoisomerase inhibition, OLED materials, bioimaging

## Preprints
- ChemRxiv (2026): The Antaki Synthesis Revisited. DOI: 10.26434/chemrxiv.15001039/v1
- Zenodo (2026): Benz[h]indeno[1,2-b]quinolines: Priority and Potential. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19428030

## Pages
- / — Biography, recognition, publications, timeline
- /synthesis-1963.html — The Antaki synthesis detail, reaction conditions, patents
- /synthesis-1967.html — Pentacyclic fluorescent compounds, structural complexity, modern literature gap

## About this site
An independent scholarly project documenting the published scientific work of H. B. F. Antaki (1923–1993), compiled by his family.
